mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the short-rate discount rate and supports equity valuations, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is falsified if rate cuts are followed by flat or negative SPX returns in most episodes.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines are not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered observations, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above the policy rate, real rates compress and the market interprets the stance as less restrictive than the nominal level suggests, which should lift SPX over the next 5 trading days. The effect should reverse if inflation is stable or falling.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises faster than FEDFUNDS over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL outpacing FEDFUNDS does not coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns in at least 60% of trigger events or if the sign flips when inflation momentum is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening labor demand and higher recession probability, so it predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when unemployment rises because of supply reclassification without broader demand deterioration.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of periods after a 0.3pp 3-month UNRATE rise, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is above 0.03 in the wrong direction.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS signals stronger nominal spending and corporate revenue growth, which should support S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon. The signal weakens if retail sales rise only because of inflation while real activity stalls.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases after adjusting for CPIAUCSL momentum.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and PAYEMS both rise together, firms are expanding output with added labor, which signals improving real activity and should push SPX higher over the next 20 trading days. If industrial production rises while payrolls contract, the signal should fail.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both rise over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in INDPRO and PAYEMS fail to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or if the signal disappears when either series is flat.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST signals housing demand destruction and tighter financial conditions for cyclicals, which should pressure equities lower over the next 60 trading days. The effect should be strongest when housing declines are not offset by stronger retail sales or employment.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST declines greater than 8% over 3 months are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle tightening environment, an increase in UNRATE combined with a decline in PAYEMS identifies broad labor-market deterioration and should predict negative SPX returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal fails if unemployment rises while payrolls remain robust.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined UNRATE-up/PAYEMS-down state does not produce negative 5-day SPX returns in at least 60% of occurrences, or if the effect vanishes outside late-cycle tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a rising FEDFUNDS rate should reduce equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when hikes are fully anticipated and inflation is falling fast enough to offset the discount-rate effect.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.50 percentage points over 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of periods following a 0.50pp 6-month FEDFUNDS increase, especially when CPIAUCSL momentum is not falling.
